card.intuition: Hash the pattern and slide a same-size hash window over the text — only compare characters when hashes match, giving average-case O(n) instead of O(nm).
card.analogy: Fingerprint-matching in a crowd: instead of checking every face feature-by-feature, you glance at a quick fingerprint sketch and only stop for a full comparison when the sketch matches.
card.time_complexity: O(n + m) average, O(nm) worst case with hash collisions
when_to_use: Single-pattern search where average-case O(n) matters and worst case is acceptable
Multi-pattern search — hash all patterns into a set, slide one window over text (O(n + Σm))
Substring hashing questions where you need equality checks on arbitrary substrings
gotchas: A hash match is necessary but not sufficient — always verify character-by-character to rule out collisions
Rolling hash arithmetic must handle modular subtraction correctly: (a - b) % MOD can go negative in Python only if you use %, but add MOD before taking mod to be safe
Choose a large prime MOD (e.g., 10⁹+7) and a base larger than your alphabet size to minimize collisions

exercise.prompt: Complete the rolling hash update inside Rabin-Karp. The old leftmost character leaves, the new character enters.
hints: After removing the old character, the window shifts right — multiply the remaining hash by BASE (shift left in polynomial) then add the new character's value
New hash = (old_hash × BASE + ord(new_char)) % MOD — same recurrence used to build the hash initially
exercise.test_code:
assert rabin_karp('abcdefg', 'cde')        == 2,  'Pattern at index 2'
assert rabin_karp('hello world', 'world')  == 6,  'Pattern at index 6'
assert rabin_karp('aaaa', 'aa')            == 0,  'Overlapping: first occurrence'
assert rabin_karp('abc', 'xyz')            == -1, 'Pattern not found'
assert rabin_karp('abc', 'abcd')           == -1, 'Pattern longer than text'
assert rabin_karp('a', 'a')               == 0,  'Single char match'
print('PASS')
